title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
HORIZON TIMES
Latest Space Highlights | March 2026

ISRO SUCCESSFULLY TESTS POWERFUL CRYOGENIC ENGINE
The Indian Space Research Organisation recently conducted a successful hot test of the CE-20 cryogenic engine, achieving a thrust level of 22 tonnes. The test was carried out at the ISRO Propulsion Complex and marks a major step forward for India's heavy-lift launch capabilities.
Cryogenic engines are extremely important for modern rockets because they provide higher efficiency and allow rockets

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
tant for modern rockets because they provide higher efficiency and allow rockets to carry heavier payloads into orbit. This development strengthens India's launch vehicle technology and supports upcoming missions that require greater payload capacity.

INDIA PLANS FUTURE MOON MISSIONS: CHANDRAYAAN-4 AND 5
Following the success of Chandrayaan-3, ISRO has revealed plans for Chandrayaan-4 and Chandrayaan-5, two ambitious missions aimed at expanding India's lunar exploration program.
Chandrayaan-4 i

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
s missions aimed at expanding India's lunar exploration program.
Chandrayaan-4 is expected to focus on collecting lunar samples and bringing them back to Earth, a major technological challenge attempted by only a few space agencies. Chandrayaan-5 may include a heavier 350-kg rover with a mission life of about 100 days, enabling longer and more detailed scientific studies on the Moon's surface.

NASA SATELLITE RETURNS TO EARTH AFTER 14 YEARS
After spending nearly 14 years studying Earth's radiati

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
TO EARTH AFTER 14 YEARS
After spending nearly 14 years studying Earth's radiation belts, NASA's Van Allen Probe A re-entered Earth's atmosphere in March 2026.
The spacecraft burned up mostly during reentry over the Pacific Ocean, posing almost no risk to people on Earth. During its mission, the probe helped scientists understand space weather and the behavior of charged particles around Earth, discoveries that continue to influence satellite design and astronaut safety.

NASA BEGINS BUILDING DR

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
nue to influence satellite design and astronaut safety.

NASA BEGINS BUILDING DRAGONFLY MISSION TO TITAN
NASA has started building Dragonfly, a nuclear-powered rotorcraft that will explore Titan, Saturn's largest moon.
Scheduled for launch in 2028, Dragonfly will fly between different locations on Titan's surface to study its chemistry, atmosphere, and geology. Scientists believe Titan may contain complex organic molecules that could provide clues about how life might begin on other worlds.

SPA

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
ecules that could provide clues about how life might begin on other worlds.

SPACE FACT OF THE ISSUE
Titan is one of the most Earth-like worlds in the solar system. It has rivers, lakes, clouds, and rain but instead of water, these features are made of liquid methane and ethane. Because Titan has a thick atmosphere and complex chemistry, scientists believe it may resemble what early Earth looked like billions of years ago, making it a key location for studying the origins of life.

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
s ago, making it a key location for studying the origins of life.

QUICK ORBIT UPDATE
Space agencies worldwide are increasing investments in Moon exploration and deep-space missions. New propulsion technologies such as advanced cryogenic engines are improving rocket efficiency. Robotic explorers like Dragonfly are opening a new era of flying exploration on other worlds.

title: horizon_sponsorship
text:

title: horizon_sponsorship

Partnering with Team Horizon offers your organi

title: tedx_speaker_intro
text:
Starting as a camera assistant in Thiruvananthapuram to becoming one of Malayalam cinema's most influential cinematographers, Sujith Vasudev has painted stories in light and shadow in films like Drishyam, Memories, 7th Day and Lucifer. His works did not just capture scenes, but captured feeling.
At TEDxCUSAT GENESIS, he steps out from behind the lens to reflect on beginnings, how ideas are born, how a single thought evolves into something that moves us, and how every frame we see started long be

title: tedx_speaker_intro
text:
evolves into something that moves us, and how every frame we see started long before the camera rolled.
This is not just about cinema. It is about the first spark, the first choice, the genesis of vision itself and the courage to share it.

text:
HORIZON TIMES
Latest Space Highlights | March 2026

ISRO SUCCESSFULLY TESTS POWERFUL CRYOGENIC ENGINE
The Indian Space Research Organisation recently conducted a successful hot test of the CE-20 cryogenic engine, achieving a thrust level of 22 tonnes. The test was carried out at the ISRO Propulsion Complex and marks a major step forward for India's heavy-lift launch capabilities.
Cryogenic engines are extremely important for modern rockets because they provide higher efficiency and allow rockets

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
tant for modern rockets because they provide higher efficiency and allow rockets to carry heavier payloads into orbit. This development strengthens India's launch vehicle technology and supports upcoming missions that require greater payload capacity.

INDIA PLANS FUTURE MOON MISSIONS: CHANDRAYAAN-4 AND 5
Following the success of Chandrayaan-3, ISRO has revealed plans for Chandrayaan-4 and Chandrayaan-5, two ambitious missions aimed at expanding India's lunar exploration program.
Chandrayaan-4 i

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
s missions aimed at expanding India's lunar exploration program.
Chandrayaan-4 is expected to focus on collecting lunar samples and bringing them back to Earth, a major technological challenge attempted by only a few space agencies. Chandrayaan-5 may include a heavier 350-kg rover with a mission life of about 100 days, enabling longer and more detailed scientific studies on the Moon's surface.

NASA SATELLITE RETURNS TO EARTH AFTER 14 YEARS
After spending nearly 14 years studying Earth's radiati

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
TO EARTH AFTER 14 YEARS
After spending nearly 14 years studying Earth's radiation belts, NASA's Van Allen Probe A re-entered Earth's atmosphere in March 2026.
The spacecraft burned up mostly during reentry over the Pacific Ocean, posing almost no risk to people on Earth. During its mission, the probe helped scientists understand space weather and the behavior of charged particles around Earth, discoveries that continue to influence satellite design and astronaut safety.

NASA BEGINS BUILDING DR

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
nue to influence satellite design and astronaut safety.

NASA BEGINS BUILDING DRAGONFLY MISSION TO TITAN
NASA has started building Dragonfly, a nuclear-powered rotorcraft that will explore Titan, Saturn's largest moon.
Scheduled for launch in 2028, Dragonfly will fly between different locations on Titan's surface to study its chemistry, atmosphere, and geology. Scientists believe Titan may contain complex organic molecules that could provide clues about how life might begin on other worlds.

SPA

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
ecules that could provide clues about how life might begin on other worlds.

SPACE FACT OF THE ISSUE
Titan is one of the most Earth-like worlds in the solar system. It has rivers, lakes, clouds, and rain but instead of water, these features are made of liquid methane and ethane. Because Titan has a thick atmosphere and complex chemistry, scientists believe it may resemble what early Earth looked like billions of years ago, making it a key location for studying the origins of life.

text:
HORIZON TIMES
Latest Space Highlights | March 2026

ISRO SUCCESSFULLY TESTS POWERFUL CRYOGENIC ENGINE
The Indian Space Research Organisation recently conducted a successful hot test of the CE-20 cryogenic engine, achieving a thrust level of 22 tonnes. The test was carried out at the ISRO Propulsion Complex and marks a major step forward for India's heavy-lift launch capabilities.
Cryogenic engines are extremely important for modern rockets because they provide higher efficiency and allow rockets

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
tant for modern rockets because they provide higher efficiency and allow rockets to carry heavier payloads into orbit. This development strengthens India's launch vehicle technology and supports upcoming missions that require greater payload capacity.

INDIA PLANS FUTURE MOON MISSIONS: CHANDRAYAAN-4 AND 5
Following the success of Chandrayaan-3, ISRO has revealed plans for Chandrayaan-4 and Chandrayaan-5, two ambitious missions aimed at expanding India's lunar exploration program.
Chandrayaan-4 i

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
s missions aimed at expanding India's lunar exploration program.
Chandrayaan-4 is expected to focus on collecting lunar samples and bringing them back to Earth, a major technological challenge attempted by only a few space agencies. Chandrayaan-5 may include a heavier 350-kg rover with a mission life of about 100 days, enabling longer and more detailed scientific studies on the Moon's surface.

NASA SATELLITE RETURNS TO EARTH AFTER 14 YEARS
After spending nearly 14 years studying Earth's radiati

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
TO EARTH AFTER 14 YEARS
After spending nearly 14 years studying Earth's radiation belts, NASA's Van Allen Probe A re-entered Earth's atmosphere in March 2026.
The spacecraft burned up mostly during reentry over the Pacific Ocean, posing almost no risk to people on Earth. During its mission, the probe helped scientists understand space weather and the behavior of charged particles around Earth, discoveries that continue to influence satellite design and astronaut safety.

NASA BEGINS BUILDING DR

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
nue to influence satellite design and astronaut safety.

NASA BEGINS BUILDING DRAGONFLY MISSION TO TITAN
NASA has started building Dragonfly, a nuclear-powered rotorcraft that will explore Titan, Saturn's largest moon.
Scheduled for launch in 2028, Dragonfly will fly between different locations on Titan's surface to study its chemistry, atmosphere, and geology. Scientists believe Titan may contain complex organic molecules that could provide clues about how life might begin on other worlds.

SPA

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
ecules that could provide clues about how life might begin on other worlds.

SPACE FACT OF THE ISSUE
Titan is one of the most Earth-like worlds in the solar system. It has rivers, lakes, clouds, and rain but instead of water, these features are made of liquid methane and ethane. Because Titan has a thick atmosphere and complex chemistry, scientists believe it may resemble what early Earth looked like billions of years ago, making it a key location for studying the origins of life.

text:
HORIZON TIMES
Latest Space Highlights | March 2026

ISRO SUCCESSFULLY TESTS POWERFUL CRYOGENIC ENGINE
The Indian Space Research Organisation recently conducted a successful hot test of the CE-20 cryogenic engine, achieving a thrust level of 22 tonnes. The test was carried out at the ISRO Propulsion Complex and marks a major step forward for India's heavy-lift launch capabilities.
Cryogenic engines are extremely important for modern rockets because they provide higher efficiency and allow rockets

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
tant for modern rockets because they provide higher efficiency and allow rockets to carry heavier payloads into orbit. This development strengthens India's launch vehicle technology and supports upcoming missions that require greater payload capacity.

INDIA PLANS FUTURE MOON MISSIONS: CHANDRAYAAN-4 AND 5
Following the success of Chandrayaan-3, ISRO has revealed plans for Chandrayaan-4 and Chandrayaan-5, two ambitious missions aimed at expanding India's lunar exploration program.
Chandrayaan-4 i

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
s missions aimed at expanding India's lunar exploration program.
Chandrayaan-4 is expected to focus on collecting lunar samples and bringing them back to Earth, a major technological challenge attempted by only a few space agencies. Chandrayaan-5 may include a heavier 350-kg rover with a mission life of about 100 days, enabling longer and more detailed scientific studies on the Moon's surface.

NASA SATELLITE RETURNS TO EARTH AFTER 14 YEARS
After spending nearly 14 years studying Earth's radiati

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
TO EARTH AFTER 14 YEARS
After spending nearly 14 years studying Earth's radiation belts, NASA's Van Allen Probe A re-entered Earth's atmosphere in March 2026.
The spacecraft burned up mostly during reentry over the Pacific Ocean, posing almost no risk to people on Earth. During its mission, the probe helped scientists understand space weather and the behavior of charged particles around Earth, discoveries that continue to influence satellite design and astronaut safety.

NASA BEGINS BUILDING DR

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
nue to influence satellite design and astronaut safety.

NASA BEGINS BUILDING DRAGONFLY MISSION TO TITAN
NASA has started building Dragonfly, a nuclear-powered rotorcraft that will explore Titan, Saturn's largest moon.
Scheduled for launch in 2028, Dragonfly will fly between different locations on Titan's surface to study its chemistry, atmosphere, and geology. Scientists believe Titan may contain complex organic molecules that could provide clues about how life might begin on other worlds.

SPA

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
ecules that could provide clues about how life might begin on other worlds.

SPACE FACT OF THE ISSUE
Titan is one of the most Earth-like worlds in the solar system. It has rivers, lakes, clouds, and rain but instead of water, these features are made of liquid methane and ethane. Because Titan has a thick atmosphere and complex chemistry, scientists believe it may resemble what early Earth looked like billions of years ago, making it a key location for studying the origins of life.

title: newsletter_horizon_times
text:
s ago, making it a key location for studying the origins of life.

QUICK ORBIT UPDATE
Space agencies worldwide are increasing investments in Moon exploration and deep-space missions. New propulsion technologies such as advanced cryogenic engines are improving rocket efficiency. Robotic explorers like Dragonfly are opening a new era of flying exploration on other worlds.

title: horizon_minutes
text:
TEAM HORIZON | Minutes of the meet

This week was more about consistency than big milestones.
The mechanical team continued working on suspension refinement. Most of the progress came from small iterations — testing, adjusting, and repeating the process.
On the electronics side, integration is still ongoing. A few issues came up during testing, but they helped us identify what needs to be improved.
We also started focusing more on documentation and how our work aligns with higher TRL levels.

title: horizon_sponsorship_letter
text:
Subject: Sponsorship Opportunity with Team Horizon CUSAT

Hi there,
Hope you're doing well!

We're reaching out from Team Horizon, a student team from the Cochin University of Science and Technology (CUSAT) that designs and builds fully autonomous Mars rovers. Our latest rover, Tiranga 2.0, has been developed entirely in-house and is capable of performing tasks such as autonomous navigation, terrain exploration, sampling, and system diagnostics in simulated Martian environments.

Over the past y

title: horizon_sponsorship_letter
text:
ling, and system diagnostics in simulated Martian environments.

Over the past year, our journey has taken us to the global stage. We represented India at the European Rover Challenge 2024 in Kraków, Poland, where we secured an 18th global rank, proudly standing as the only team from South India at the event.

Continuing this momentum, we also participated in the International Rover Challenge 2026, where we achieved a world rank of 25. These milestones reflect the consistency, technical depth, a

title: horizon_sponsorship_letter
text:

title: assignment_rsa
text:
RSA is a public key cryptographic algorithm used for secure communication.
It is based on the mathematical properties of large prime numbers. Two prime numbers are selected and used to generate public and private keys.
The process includes key generation, encryption, and decryption.
The security of RSA depends on the difficulty of factoring large numbers, making it suitable for modern encryption systems.

Latest Space Highlights | March 2026

ISRO TESTS CE-20 CRYOGENIC ENGINE
ISRO recently carried out a successful hot test of the CE-20 cryogenic engine, reaching a thrust of around 22 tonnes.
Cryogenic engines are complex to work with, but they offer higher efficiency, especially for missions that need to carry heavier payloads. This test is another step towards improving India's heavy-lift capabilities.

WHAT'S NEXT AFTER CHANDRAYAAN-3?
After the success of Chandrayaan-3, ISRO is already planning ahead.
Future missions like Chandrayaan-4 and 5 are expected to focus on more advanced objectives, including the possibility of bringing lunar samples back to Earth and deploying heavier rovers for extended missions.

DRAGONFLY: A MISSION TO TITAN
NASA has started building Dragonfly, a rotorcraft designed to explore Titan.
Unlike traditional rovers, Dragonfly will be able to fly between loca

title: newsletter_issue01
text:
ore Titan.
Unlike traditional rovers, Dragonfly will be able to fly between locations, making exploration more flexible. Titan's complex chemistry makes it an interesting place to study the origins of life.

SPACE FACT OF THE ISSUE
Titan has rivers and lakes, but they are made of liquid methane instead of water. It is one of the most Earth-like environments in the solar system, despite being extremely different.
